content: He is a four armed character who doesn't even use the extra arms (holding back).
He is an M1 merchant whose R special is a glorified (Omni directional) naoya dash with a long ass Cooldown.
His more noteworthy power is his evasive bait.
Downright the best and safest evasive bait in the game.
After landing an M1, use 4 or 3 and press R, aim up alwyas
Basically if your enemy doesnt use their rc you get another combo.
Also landing 4 or 3 lets you use (right after) your R special, no matter if it's on COOLDOWN or not.
Making this a constant thing.
His only weakness is that he has absolutely zero neutral,
[color=hsl(300, 100%, 83%)]BUT[/color] he is an M1 merchant who works consistently,
when you land a m1 you gain the most out of everyone else (40 DMG and your evasive out)
overview.author: Nickolas4F

content: Honored One also has decent bait and he is versatile with his kit, red, tp, rappid with also somewhat the same cooldowns
Ten Shadows has an overall versatile kit for M1 trades, baiting evasive. His entire kit works in every way
Stockpile is clutching up, even if Perfection has worse bait,
he has chip DMG and just as fast cds.
Again if they don't know how to use stockpile, you win if they do you lose
Blood Manipulator has orbs where he doesn't really care about
M1 trades and makes your approach game way harder
Even though you got a lot mobility as Defense Attorney,
a good Locust Guy who won't be easily overwhelmed and reacts to your mixups.
Locust Guy will just beat you as an M1 merchant due to his DMG and bait
Aka needs to be better at the game
Cursed Partners has neutral as of outburst, second wind catching whiffed m1s,
blackflash as of free DMG when you burn a side dash and rika smash/haymaker.
He is better at M1 trades than you, even if he has less bait than you, he can deal unpunishable DMG.
The only way Cursed Partners can win is your fast cds compared to his slow CDs.
Puppet Master has booston and ultra spin offload for M1 trades while heat emission to catch you of neutral to a full combo.
Slaughtered because Disaster Plant can zone you and you can't do shit about it.
You get hard zoned and stalled, he has insane safe DMG from landing an M1 too, basically better than you
The better M1 merchant winds
She has better neutral, better m1 tools, better effective range,
neutral, only her evasive bait is mid and how she is an M1 merhcant before she gets mass,
but she has chip and should win.
One sided slaughter, Mangaka R special denied your existence as an M1 merchant.
Forcing you to lose your side dash and unable to approach,
even if he is worse than you in M1 trades, he has r aerial 2 into 1 and 4 to chip you down,
even If Mangaka chip is lame, you don't have anything
One of the better characters to dealing with M1 merchants,
Lucky Coward should be beating you.
Only issue is crushing jaws having Mele iframes after the third hit.
But you're fine either way
Black Death has chip, his detach forces an M1 trade where if you win you gain a lot.
But he can still zone with his R special and is better at dealing with M1 merchants due to parry and inane chip DMG from zoning
You can spit people like Salaryman when he is charging up.
To deny some neutral and put the DMG debuff (it's nothing crazy really)
It basically makes you a 100 hp character for 13 seconds
black-mucus.author: Nickolas4F

content: You can downslam and press 4, since it's grab,
if they evasive while grabbed they remain grabbed and just burned their ragdol cancel.
wing-throw.author: Nickolas4F

content: Aim up when you do it, so you get more height of it to roll or for your frontdash to be harder to check
fluttering-pounce.author: Nickolas4F

content: If you decide to not use your R special in neutral and you have it off cd,
you can surf after an uppercut and press R to start and air combo (it will just do more DMG overall).
